rulings.Case Title: DOORDASH, INC. VS. 901 MARKET ST SF LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Portions Of Its Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication And Documents In Support Thereof; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 23, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF DOORDASH, INC.'s Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Portions Of Its Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR, notice of bankruptcy stay on file. Notice may be re-set for hearing upon resolution on bankruptcy proceeding if necessary and proper. Filings subject to this motion will remain conditionally under seal until further order of the Court. Moving party shall pick up courtesy copies of sealed documents by no later than March 3, 2021 (any weekday between 9:00 a.m. and 10:00 a.m.) Courtesy copies will be destroyed on March 4, 2021. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/VP)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL E. HINOA ET AL VS. 2154 TAYLOR LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 23, 2021 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANTS SAMUEL HIONA, CATHLEEN THOMPSON, DENNIS THOMPSON, BETH BLEDSOE and ANA GANOVIC'S DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for damages due to unauthorized entry/possession/use of the cross-complainant's property. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/VP)
